name: Estivella - Penya Roja Trail
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra Calderona — Estivella-Penya Roja (PR-CV 369)
description: Hike the difficult 15.2-mile Estivella - Penya Roja Trail in Sierra Calderona Natural Park, gaining 2537 feet over 7 hours.
region_intro: Hiking around Estivella offers exploration within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, a region characterized by a complex relief of small valleys, ravines, and numerous peaks. The landscape features rugged rocks and abundant leafy vegetation, alongside picturesque orange groves in the surrounding countryside. Prominent natural features include the El Garbí viewpoint, a 601-meter rocky ledge providing panoramic vistas, and the Font del Barraix, a favored spot with a natural water spring. Best hiking trails around Estivella The most popular hiking route is Natural Park of Serra Calderona — Estivella-Penya Roja (PR-CV 369), a 15.2 miles (24.4 km) trail that takes 7 hours 31 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the diverse terrain of the Parc Natural de la Serra Calderona. Another top favourite among local hikers is Garbí Viewpoint – El Garbí Recreation Area loop from Segart, a moderate 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This route leads to the El Garbí viewpoint, offering extensive panoramic views. Local hikers also love the Beselga Castle – Barraix Trail loop from Estivella, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra Calderona and past Beselga Castle, often completed in about 3 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Estivella is defined by the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, including rugged peaks, deep ravines, and lush valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult ascents. The routes in Estivella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Estivella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Estivella?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes around Estivella, offering a diverse range of experiences within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. These include options for various fitness levels, from moderate paths to more difficult ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Estivella?
A: Estivella benefits from a mild climate, making hiking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winters are mild.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Estivella?
A: The terrain around Estivella, particularly within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, is characterized by a complex relief of small valleys, ravines, and numerous peaks. You'll encounter rugged rocks and abundant leafy vegetation, with some routes also passing through picturesque orange groves.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks to see on the trails?
A: Yes, a prominent highlight is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Garbí Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, a spectacular rocky ledge offering breathtaking panoramic vistas over the Mediterranean Sea and the Morvedre Valley. Other points of interest include the natural water spring at Font del Barraix and historical sites like Beselga Castle. You can also find other peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1044538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tristán&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mola de Segart&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites can I discover while hiking near Estivella?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, some hiking routes allow you to encounter historical elements such as ancient Arab irrigation systems and traditional Medieval-style houses. Within Estivella itself, you can visit the Els Arcs aqueduct, the Baroque church of Santos Juanes, and an interesting parish museum.

Q: Are there any natural water features or lakes to explore?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3819656/lakes-around-estivella" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Barraix&lt;/a&gt; is a particularly beautiful part of the mountains, featuring a natural water spring that is a favored spot for walkers and cyclists. While large lakes are not a primary feature directly on the trails, you can find smaller ponds like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2653169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pond on the El Berro Trail&lt;/a&gt; and other interesting water features in the wider region.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Estivella?
A: The trails in Estivella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, including its rugged peaks, deep ravines, and lush valleys, as well as the stunning panoramic views from spots like El Garbí.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes around Estivella are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3962186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbí Viewpoint – El Garbí Recreation Area loop from Segart&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31704112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beselga Castle – Barraix Trail loop from Estivella&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the Sierra Calderona and past historical sites.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Estivella?
A: While many trails in the Sierra Calderona Natural Park can be challenging, there are easier options suitable for families. Look for routes described as 'easy' or 'moderate' with shorter distances and less elevation gain. The region's mild climate and picturesque orange groves also make for pleasant, less strenuous walks.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Estivella?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, but it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock. Be mindful of other hikers and ensure you clean up after your pet.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Estivella?
A: Yes, parking is typically available in or near Estivella and at common starting points for trails within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route descriptions or local signage.

Q: How can I reach the trailheads using public transport?
A: Estivella is a small municipality, and while local buses might connect it to larger towns in Valencia province, direct public transport to specific trailheads within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park can be limited. It's best to check local bus schedules for connections to Estivella and then plan for a short walk or taxi to the desired trailhead.

Q: Are there any easy hikes for beginners?
A: Yes, among the 22 available routes, there are 2 classified as 'easy' and 17 as 'moderate'. These shorter, less strenuous paths are ideal for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3962186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbí Viewpoint – El Garbí Recreation Area loop from Segart&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.0-mile path that offers great views without extreme difficulty.

Q: Where can I find places to eat or stay after a hike?
A: Estivella itself offers local amenities including cafes and restaurants where you can refuel after your hike. For accommodation, you'll find options within the village or in nearby towns, ranging from guesthouses to small hotels, catering to visitors exploring the Sierra Calderona Natural Park.
tips: The castle stretched from east to west following the elongated shape of the rocky base where it sits. The tower stood at the west end, was made of rammed earth and had 3 floors, and it is possibly the Arab tower. Next to the tower was the residential building, two stories high, and of which only remains of its walls remain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Estivella - Penya Roja Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied and scenic experience within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. You'll encounter mountains with rugged rocks and lush vegetation. The route includes both wider tracks and narrower paths (sendas), featuring significant ascents and descents. One section crosses the Barranc de Linares (Linares Ravine).

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, the Estivella - Penya Roja Trail is rated as difficult and requires very good fitness. It's considered an intermediate route, and hikers need to be sure-footed due to the varied terrain and elevation changes. It is not recommended for beginners or young children.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers several points of interest. You can visit Beselga Castle. From elevated points like El Garbí, which is near the trail, you can enjoy breathtaking views over the Mediterranean Sea. The 'Penya Roja' (Red Rock) itself is a significant landmark, known for its vertical wall of calcareous materials that glow with reddish hues at sunset.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see in Sierra Calderona Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Calderona Natural Park is rich in wildlife. Hikers have opportunities to spot various birds, including Bonelli's eagles, goshawks, and eagle owls. Smaller mammals like rabbits and squirrels are also common, and you might even see Spanish pond turtles in water sources.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be hiked year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, making early morning starts essential, and winters can bring cooler weather, though snow is rare. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Is the Estivella - Penya Roja Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the trail. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife in the Sierra Calderona Natural Park and to ensure the safety and comfort of other hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Sierra Calderona Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits required or entrance fees to access the Sierra Calderona Natural Park or to hike the Estivella - Penya Roja Trail. However, as it is a natural park, please respect all regulations regarding waste, noise, and wildlife protection.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail officially known as PR-CV 369 starts near Estivella. Parking is typically available in or near the town of Estivella, which serves as a common access point for routes within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. Specific parking areas might be signposted for hikers.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Estivella - Penya Roja Trail (PR-CV 369) is part of a network of trails. It intersects with sections of other official routes such as the [CIMA VA10] Alto del Garbí * Estivella, De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa 02), and the Vía Verde Ojos Negros. You'll also find parts of the Camí de Barraix and Camí de la Font de l'Omet along the way.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, it's essential to be well-prepared. Bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months, as water sources may be limited. Wear sturdy hiking boots, appropriate clothing for the weather, sun protection, and carry a map or GPS device. Snacks and a first-aid kit are also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any historical or archaeological features on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Penya Roja' itself holds historical significance. Archaeological remains, including ancient cave paintings and engravings, have been discovered at its base, indicating continuous human habitation in the area since prehistoric times.
